cache.source_policy: Refreshed from FRED public CSV endpoints. Homepage build reads this cache and does not fetch FRED live.

doctrine.primary_rule: Render raw credit spread data first; text labels are secondary.
doctrine.purpose: Expose whether borrower trust is broadening, fragile, or deteriorating.
doctrine.interpretation_position: Secondary to spread and bank-credit evidence.

macro_gate_contribution.caveat: Mission 5C covers spread stress and bank credit growth. Default rates, lending standards, HYG/LQD price trend, and private-credit stress remain missing evidence.

source.label: ICE BofA US High Yield OAS
ICE BofA CCC & Lower US High Yield OAS

bank_credit_yoy.label: Bank credit YoY growth
bank_credit_yoy.method: Year-over-year percentage change in bank credit seed series.

web_summary.lead: Credit shows whether the system trusts repayment enough for risk to broaden.
web_summary.current_read: HY 2.67%, IG 0.76%, CCC 9.64%, bank credit YoY null%.

missing_evidence: Senior Loan Officer Survey / lending standards

analysis.statement: Credit trust deteriorates when spreads widen and weakest-credit stress rises; benign spreads support broader risk only if bank credit is not contracting.
analysis.invalidation: Do not upgrade risk permission if HY/CCC spreads rise materially or bank credit growth turns negative.

limitations: Default rates, lending standards, HYG/LQD price trend, and private-credit stress remain missing evidence.
